I think Nolan's signature non-linear timeline actually hurt this film rather than make it excel like it does for other Christopher Nolan films. And that's just because I have to buy into this rivalry and I think a linear timeline would help with that. Because it's sometimes confusing when you have to keep track of when everything takes place and try to align that with decisions characters make. Other than that, this film is great! It has one of the best plot-twist endings, has great characters, clear themes, and great writing throughout!

Overall a very enjoyable film, shows the true cost of obsession. I always love a good twist and I thinl this one was done very well with clues throughout the film. Some scenes had slow pacing and I found that the dialog was mumbled sometimes.

A meticulously constructed descent into obsession, where suspense and mystery coil tighter with every revelation. Anchored by compelling, morally eroding characters and guided by the director’s razor-sharp vision, the film transforms illusion into tragedy, proving that the greatest tricks are not what we see, but what we’re willing to sacrifice to believe.
The movie as a whole is big and after a while you lose interest , because the plot and the pace are monotonous, There is a good twist in it and the directing is enough.

Christopher Nolan really crafted a masterpiece here. It’s one of those rare films that demands a second watch the moment the credits roll, specifically because that "unpredictable" twist recontextualizes every single scene that came before it.​​The Price of Obsession​it isn't just a magic movie " It’s a psychological thriller about obsession. The rivalry between Robert Angier and Alfred Borden isn't just professional; it’s destructive. It shows that to truly "outdo" a rival, these men were willing to sacrifice their sanity, their families, and ultimately, their humanity.​The "Prestige" of the Title​The way the film explains the three parts of a magic trick—The Pledge, The Turn, and The Prestige—is brilliant. It doesn't just name the movie; it provides the literal blueprint for how the story is told. The film itself is the magic trick.​

This is my favorite movie of all time. The twist is the best of Nolan’s filmography in my opinion. Bale and Jackman characterize their roles perfectly. Music is great. Subject is engaging (magicians.)
Yes. Kept you guessing the entire movie and then a great twist at the end!!!

Definitivamente la recomiendo, es muy entretenida y te mantiene enganchado todo el tiempo, solamente evita spoilers, por favor!!! Genio, Christopher Nolan!

Production: 10/10 - Christopher Nolan doesn't miss - Christian Bale and Hugh Jackman did a great job - Novel and incredible plot line with the perfect addition of sci-fi to this revenge film Plot: 10/10 - Unbelievable plot twists - Buildup was near perfect Cinema: 10/10 - Was hooked from the start - Felt pure shock at plot twists - Motives of character actions were perfectly understandable, could connect with all characters Memorability: 10/10 - Top 10 movie endings oat - Even with the complex plot, could recall every single event after watching because of the significance of each scene Do recommend

Good, even flashy at times, but ultimately the film places tricking the viewer far above telling the characters' stories, which is I suppose the very definition of what a magician does. I felt like, other than obsession as motivation, I didn't know much about these characters.
This is simply on of the best piece of art i have seen. The atmosphere, the story and with every fram turning you see a new hero. This is really a masterpiece to remember.

Story: 10/10 – A brilliantly crafted tale of obsession, rivalry, and sacrifice. Every twist hits hard, and the final reveal is unforgettable. Atmosphere/Visuals: 9.5/10 – Dark, Victorian-era style that feels mysterious and elegant. Nolan builds tension in every scene. Soundtrack: 9/10 – Haunting, subtle, and perfectly fitting. It slowly builds the sense of unease throughout the film. Concept: 10/10 – Magicians, illusions, science, rivalry—one of Nolan’s smartest and most unique premises. Performances: 10/10 – Hugh Jackman and Christian Bale are both at their best. Their chemistry and intensity carry the whole movie. David Bowie as Tesla is a perfect touch. Overall, 9.6/10
Genius. Great film led by great acting.

Arguably the best movie of all time. The story is absolutely phenomenal, as is the acting. Perfect movie

Love a movie with a good twist. This movie is one of the great ones. A story of obsession, ego, and lethal ambition can feel farfetched but I believe that it's something anyone can fall into. It's well acted and the leads carry it past when the plot wavers, but it thankfully does not overinflate itself with needless fluff. This is a movie I love to recommend as we all do with twist movies, though it doesn't call out for viewing as much as other films.

Almost everything is great with the film and I highly recommend you watch it, but Nolan somehow found a way to make Scarlett Johansson look bad. She is horribly miscast and if her atrocious English accent weren't enough she just didn't look convincing in her costumes - late 19th century garb, nor do her natural mannerisms make for a believable character of the type she is playing. She did her best, but man oh man she's never looked worse in a role. Other than her, the movie is great.

This was an amazing movie by Christopher Nolan the story is so good and a lot of twist and turns happen and the ending has one of the best plot twists of all time easily, the story was good and this is one of my favorite Nolan movies hands down.

Good cast and an interesting, “real” perspective on the magicians and their industry in the 19th century that lends itself to an entertaining one-upping match between Jackman and Bale. Had Nolan kept with a more grounded story that kept the nitty-gritty and ruthless competitive energy of the players in the industry at the forefront, I think it would have led to a more satisfying dramatic ending that would have arrived our characters at a natural point befitting their morally questionable actions and the desires that fueled them.

There are parts of the film that are a bit confusing but the ending makes up for it in one of the best endings I have ever seen. My final grade A-
